name: Michoacan (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field and details of the arms

description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Michoacan centred.
history: Michoacan has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.

The arms are quartered: the upper left shows a fish on water, recalling the lake-fishing P'urhepecha heritage and the original meaning of "Michoacan" ("place of the fishermen"); the upper right depicts the equestrian statue of independence hero Jose Maria Morelos; the lower left shows a horseman recalling the conquistadors; the lower right shows a book and torch for the state's reputation as a cradle of Mexican learning. An inescutcheon honours Vasco de Quiroga, the 16th-century bishop and reformer.
